Summary: Portal Access, Sign In, and Role Routing
Description: Build a reliable identity and entry experience for provider and member personas so the portal always routes users into the correct onboarding or dashboard path.

Summary: As a returning provider, I want sign in to route me directly to the provider dashboard so that I do not re-enter onboarding.
Description: Implement provider sign-in logic that validates stored role state and routes authenticated provider users to the provider dashboard and shell.

Summary: As a returning member, I want sign in to route me to the correct member destination based on enrollment status so that incomplete enrollments can be resumed.
Description: Implement member sign-in routing that sends fully enrolled users to the member dashboard and incomplete users back into the enrollment flow.

Summary: As a first-time provider, I want get-started entry to send me into provider verification rather than member enrollment so that onboarding matches my persona.
Description: Separate provider onboarding from member enrollment and route providers into credential and verification steps before workspace access.

Summary: As a first-time member, I want get-started entry to send me into the member enrollment wizard so that coverage activation happens in the right sequence.
Description: Route first-time members into the multi-step enrollment wizard with registration, KYC, plan selection, eligibility, profile, payment, and activation.

Summary: As a user switching between roles in a demo environment, I want role state to reset predictably so that provider and member screens do not bleed into each other.
Description: Create role reset and role-switch controls for demo and QA use so that role-linked navigation and saved state can be cleared safely.

Summary: Shared AI Shell, Voice, File Intake, and Human Handoff
Description: Deliver a persistent AI-first interaction layer that is available on every relevant provider and member screen with support for text, voice, file uploads, and human escalation.

Summary: As a portal user, I want a persistent Nventr assistant on all core screens so that I can ask for help without leaving my current workflow.
Description: Implement a shared shell component that injects the assistant into provider and member workflows and keeps prompt context aligned to the current page.

Summary: As a user, I want to use voice prompts so that I can trigger actions hands-free when I am multitasking or on a call.
Description: Add browser-based voice capture for dashboard prompts and assistant prompts with proper status messaging and language preference support.

Summary: As a user, I want to attach files to Nventr so that the AI can act on claim, prior auth, appeal, and billing workflows using supporting records.
Description: Support file intake metadata in the assistant and preserve attachment context when workflows are queued or handed off.

Summary: As a user, I want the assistant to queue actions instead of only routing me to forms so that the portal feels AI-first rather than menu-first.
Description: Create a shared workflow queue pattern for common intents like claims, prior auth, appointments, enrollment, payment review, and multilingual support.

Summary: As a user, I want a human handoff option so that unresolved AI interactions can be escalated with full context.
Description: Implement a handoff request flow that captures the active page, prompt, role, and attachments for handover to a live specialist.

Summary: As an operations team member, I want settings for voice replies, preferred language, and legacy-mode visibility so that the AI experience can be tuned per environment.
Description: Add a shared settings surface that stores voice reply, preferred language, and whether legacy workflows remain prominently visible.

Summary: Provider Dashboard and Operational Intelligence
Description: Create an AI-first provider command center that combines operational metrics, next-best actions, and workflow launch points for revenue cycle, quality, and member operations.

Summary: As a provider operations lead, I want a dashboard prompt bar so that I can ask for claim, auth, payment, and member insights directly from the landing screen.
Description: Add a provider dashboard prompt surface with text, voice, suggested prompts, and action status messaging.

Summary: As a provider executive, I want operational intelligence metrics for coding yield, denials, MLR, MIPS, star ratings, and predictive UM so that I can prioritize action.
Description: Build an intelligence section with production-ready metric tiles, narrative summaries, and recommended actions tied to provider goals.

Summary: As a provider analyst, I want AI-generated next-best actions from the dashboard so that metrics become operational work instead of passive reporting.
Description: Convert dashboard insights into AI actions that can open workflow modals for coding, payment recovery, appeals, utilization review, and member operations.

Summary: As a provider user, I want notifications and settings in the top bar across provider screens so that shell behavior is consistent.
Description: Standardize provider top-bar controls for notifications, settings, and profile with working shared panels.

Summary: As a provider operations team, I want ambient intake and live-agent readiness surfaced from the dashboard so that call-center and workflow support can start from one place.
Description: Expose ambient listening, intake summarization, and live-agent handoff as dashboard-level provider actions.

Summary: Provider Claims, Coding, Appeals, and Denials
Description: Support AI-assisted claim submission, coding suggestions, claim triage, denial prevention, and appeal preparation within a unified provider workflow.

Summary: As a biller, I want claim submission to show selected CPT and ICD codes on the same screen so that I can verify the packet before submitting.
Description: Keep suggested and manually added codes visible in a selected-code panel with validation before submit.

Summary: As a coder, I want AI-assisted autonomous coding and QA review so that missing documentation and denial risk can be detected before claim submission.
Description: Introduce a pre-claim AI workflow that reads clinical narrative, evaluates coding confidence, and highlights revenue integrity gaps.

Summary: As a claims specialist, I want denied claims to route directly into the appeal page with selected claim context so that appeal preparation starts immediately.
Description: Store claim context when appeal is triggered and hydrate the denial analysis page with claim, patient, and denial reason details.

Summary: As a claims lead, I want an AI triage experience for pending, denied, and appeal-ready claims so that the queue can be prioritized faster.
Description: Build a claim triage workflow that groups queue items by risk, suggested action, and automation confidence.

Summary: As an appeals specialist, I want AI appeal packaging with evidence and specialist handoff so that denial responses are faster and more defensible.
Description: Create an appeal workflow that packages denial reasoning, clinical counterpoints, supporting files, and human escalation into one review surface.

Summary: As a provider user, I want claim success and draft-save feedback so that I know whether my work has been preserved or sent.
Description: Show clear confirmation on save draft and submit actions for claims with persisted local or server-side state.

Summary: Provider Prior Authorization and Utilization Management
Description: Deliver AI-assisted prior authorization, utilization management, medical necessity review, and e-form packet generation for providers.

Summary: As a provider user, I want the prior authorization wizard to move through all steps reliably so that requests can be completed without dead ends.
Description: Complete the multi-step prior authorization flow with back, next, save draft, validation, and final submit behavior.

Summary: As a utilization reviewer, I want AI review of clinical orders and medical necessity so that the request packet is complete before payer submission.
Description: Build a utilization management workflow that checks clinical necessity, orders, labs, imaging, and payer criteria before final auth submission.

Summary: As a coordinator, I want AI-generated e-forms and supporting packet creation so that structured fields and documents can be prepared faster.
Description: Generate e-form packets from provider input and attached documents while preserving the wizard for manual edits.

Summary: As a provider user, I want queue-level interaction from the auth list so that I can review approved, pending, and missing-document requests faster.
Description: Make the authorization queue cards and filter actions interactive and AI-aware.

Summary: As an operations team, I want attached clinical files to feed directly into prior auth workflows so that Nventr can package evidence with fewer manual steps.
Description: Carry assistant file metadata into prior authorization workflows and packet review surfaces.

Summary: Provider Billing, EDI 835, ERA, Reconciliation, and PITL
Description: Create a provider billing and reconciliation workspace that combines payment exceptions, batch reporting, EDI/ERA review, underpayment recovery, and PITL checkpoints.

Summary: As a provider finance user, I want Generate Batch Report to produce a usable report so that I can review reconciliation work outside the portal if needed.
Description: Create a downloadable provider batch report artifact summarizing EDI 835, ERA, processed amounts, and recovery opportunity.

Summary: As a reconciliation analyst, I want Resolve Now to launch a deeper AI recovery workflow so that payment exceptions are not dead-end buttons.
Description: Bind payment exception CTAs into a dedicated workflow that covers ERA exceptions, posting gaps, co-pay mismatches, and underpayments.

Summary: As a provider finance lead, I want EDI 835 and ERA intelligence surfaced with recovery opportunity so that financial leakage can be prioritized.
Description: Add an AI workflow and metric view for remit normalization, posting completion, underpay detection, and next-best recovery action.

Summary: As an administrator, I want receipt downloads and email drafts to work so that provider finance communication is not blocked.
Description: Bind provider receipt, download, and email actions to downloadable artifacts and mail drafts with relevant context.

Summary: As an operations architect, I want PITL workflow visibility so that AI automation still respects human governance in payment administration.
Description: Create a PITL integration workflow showing where AI recommendations connect into administrative review checkpoints.

Summary: Provider Member Management and Assisted Enrollment
Description: Give providers a workspace for viewing members, tracking enrollment status, resuming drafts, and enrolling members on their behalf.

Summary: As a provider user, I want a member directory with status and plan context so that I can understand the current state of the members I manage.
Description: Build a provider member directory showing enrollment state, plan, contact, and last-updated information.

Summary: As a provider user, I want enrollment drafts to appear in the members workspace so that I can resume incomplete onboarding.
Description: Persist member enrollment drafts and expose a resume queue in the provider members page.

Summary: As a provider user, I want to launch provider-assisted member enrollment from multiple provider entry points so that enrollment is accessible where work happens.
Description: Allow new member enrollment to be started from dashboard, members page, and AI workflow entry points.

Summary: As a provider operator, I want AI workflow support around member operations so that I can collect missing enrollment data with less manual follow-up.
Description: Create a provider-side member operations workflow that summarizes missing steps, plan mapping, and activation blockers.

Summary: Member Dashboard, Coverage, and Benefits
Description: Deliver a member-first dashboard focused on coverage, benefits, activity, next steps, and intelligent guidance instead of provider operations content.

Summary: As a member, I want the dashboard to show member-focused insights and next steps so that I know what to do without deciphering provider language.
Description: Refine the member dashboard to show coverage, care, billing, claims, enrollment, and next recommended actions.

Summary: As a member, I want eligibility, benefits, coverage, and status visible on the dashboard so that I can quickly understand my plan standing.
Description: Show plan details, member ID, coverage start, eligibility state, and activation messaging in a structured dashboard section.

Summary: As a member, I want dashboard prompts for EOB, appointments, pharmacy, billing, and enrollment so that Nventr can guide me from the main landing page.
Description: Provide a member dashboard prompt bar with role-specific chips and text/voice input.

Summary: As a member, I want multilingual support cues and live-help options visible on the dashboard so that I know I can get help in a way that works for me.
Description: Surface language preference, multilingual support, and live-agent escalation options in member-facing dashboard and assistant flows.

Summary: Member Billing, HSA, EOB, Statements, and Point-of-Service Payment
Description: Support AI-guided billing, HSA account management, EOB explanation, statement support, receipts, administrator communication, and point-of-service payment guidance for members.

Summary: As a member, I want billing CTAs like pay with HSA, payment plan, concierge help, report download, and receipt download to work so that I can complete financial tasks from one screen.
Description: Ensure all member billing actions create meaningful outcomes such as notifications, downloads, modals, or state changes.

Summary: As a member, I want Email to Administrator to open a draft with the admin recipient hidden and prefilled so that I can escalate billing questions quickly.
Description: Build a member-friendly administrator email draft flow with optional CC and editable message content.

Summary: As a member, I want EOB explanations in plain language so that I can understand what was paid, denied, or still owed.
Description: Add EOB explanation behavior through Nventr and the billing experience with simple, member-safe wording.

Summary: As a member, I want statement AI support so that I can understand statement lines, download artifacts, and decide on the next payment action.
Description: Provide statement-oriented AI responses, downloads, and recommended actions in billing workflows.

Summary: As a member, I want point-of-service payment guidance so that I understand whether to use HSA, card, or a payment plan at the moment of care.
Description: Create a member AI workflow for point-of-service payment guidance tied to HSA balances and billing context.

Summary: Member Appointments, Lab Scheduling, and Care Journey
Description: Enable simple in-network appointment booking, test or lab follow-up scheduling, and care journey management for members.

Summary: As a member, I want to search in-network providers and book an appointment if a slot is available so that the flow is fast and simple.
Description: Support provider search, slot confirmation, booking success feedback, and calendar visibility from the member appointment page.

Summary: As a member, I want Nventr to schedule appointments from a natural-language request so that I do not have to navigate every booking control manually.
Description: Support AI booking prompts that search provider matches and book or suggest slots directly from the appointment page or assistant.

Summary: As a member, I want lab or test follow-up scheduling tied to appointments so that additional care steps can be handled in the same journey.
Description: Extend appointment workflows and AI prompts to support test or lab scheduling and preparation reminders.

Summary: As a member, I want the care journey and follow-up layer to remind me about pre-visit and post-visit steps so that I stay on track.
Description: Integrate care journey reminders, orchestration logic, and follow-up configuration with appointment and member dashboard context.

Summary: Member Pharmacy, Refills, Delivery, and Dependents
Description: Provide members with working refill, pharmacy network, medication support, delivery options, and dependent management workflows.

Summary: As a member, I want refill actions and success messages to update the pharmacy status table so that I know the request really went through.
Description: Wire refill-related CTAs to update on-screen status, notifications, and modal confirmations.

Summary: As a member, I want View Logic Chain to explain why the refill is allowed or blocked so that the AI experience feels trustworthy.
Description: Provide a clear AI reasoning modal for refill checks, coverage checks, and next action recommendations.

Summary: As a member, I want Change Pharmacy to show in-network options and update my selected pharmacy so that future fills go to the right place.
Description: Show in-network pharmacies in a working selection modal and persist the chosen pharmacy in member state.

Summary: As a member, I want the refill-required card to do something useful when clicked so that urgent medication actions are not hidden behind dead UI.
Description: Bind the refill-required tile into a modal that offers refill and pharmacy-change actions.

Summary: As a member, I want to add and manage dependents so that family members can be included in portal workflows.
Description: Create a dependent management flow under the member dependents area with stored household state.

Summary: Consolidated Demo, QA, Accessibility, and Release Readiness
Description: Prepare the portal for deeper implementation by maintaining a consolidated preview, performing page-by-page QA, and defining non-functional expectations.

Summary: As a product reviewer, I want a consolidated HTML build that preserves navigation between pages so that stakeholders can review the prototype from one file.
Description: Maintain the consolidated portal build script and output so all portal pages can be navigated inside a single HTML artifact.

Summary: As a QA lead, I want page-by-page broken-flow findings documented so that the team can prioritize remaining fixes after prototype hardening.
Description: Run repeatable QA sweeps against provider and member screens and convert findings into backlog-ready remediation items.

Summary: As a frontend engineer, I want consistent shell navigation and labels across provider and member pages so that the portal behaves like one product.
Description: Standardize left navigation, top controls, labels, and AI entry points across pages and personas.

Summary: As an accessibility reviewer, I want keyboard-friendly modals, prompts, and notifications so that core flows are usable beyond pointer-only interactions.
Description: Improve focus management, keyboard triggers, aria labels, and visible status messaging for assistant, workflow modals, and page banners.

Summary: As a product and engineering team, I want telemetry and event tracking stories defined so that usage of AI prompts and legacy fallbacks can be measured once the real build starts.
Description: Define event instrumentation for sign-in, prompt usage, workflow queue actions, downloads, handoffs, and fallback-to-legacy behavior.
